_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-3 Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Exposure (MDHHS Reviewed 2023-03-24). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-3: Nerve Agent / Organophosphate Exposure — MDHHS Reviewed 2023
purpose: Provide EMS treatment guidelines for patients exposed to nerve agents (GA, GB, GD, VX) or organophosphate pesticides, including use of nerve agent antidote kits (Duodote / Mark I) from CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN caches.

sections.title: Scene Safety
content: Do NOT enter contaminated area without appropriate PPE — refer to Protocol 10-1
Nerve agents are highly toxic — minimal exposure can be lethal
Decontamination (remove clothing, copious water irrigation) before EMS treatment
Activate CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN if nerve agent antidote kits needed — refer to Protocol 10-4
Notify online medical control and receiving hospital immediately

sections.title: SLUDGEM — Cholinergic Toxidrome Recognition
content: S — Salivation (excessive drooling)
L — Lacrimation (excessive tearing)
U — Urination (incontinence)
D — Defecation (incontinence)
G — GI distress (nausea, vomiting, cramping)
E — Emesis (vomiting)
M — Muscle twitching or spasm
Additional signs: bronchospasm, bronchorrhea, bradycardia, constricted pupils, seizures, loss of consciousness, apnea
Constricted pupils with SLUDGEM findings indicate nerve agent / organophosphate toxicity, but constricted pupils may be absent with localized dermal exposure

sections.title: Nerve Agent Antidote Kit Definitions
content: DUODOTE Auto-Injector Kit (standard Michigan CHEMPACK kit):
— Each kit contains: 2.1 mg Atropine + 600 mg Pralidoxime (2-PAM) in auto-injector
— Administered as a single auto-injector injection (anterolateral thigh or deltoid through clothing)
MARK I Auto-Injector Kit (older format, may be available):
— Each kit contains: 2 mg Atropine (separate injector) + 600 mg Pralidoxime (separate injector)
— Two separate auto-injectors administered sequentially
NOTE: For dosing purposes in this protocol, 1 NA kit = 1 Duodote OR 1 Mark I set

sections.title: Symptom Severity Classification
content: THRESHOLD / SELF-RESCUE: dim vision, tearing, runny nose, nausea/vomiting, abdominal cramps, or shortness of breath with positive evidence of nerve agent or organophosphate on site — Medical Control order required
MILD: threshold symptoms plus constricted pupils, muscle twitching, increased tearing/drooling/runny nose, diaphoresis, nausea/vomiting, abdominal cramps, or diarrhea — Medical Control order required
MODERATE: SLUDGEM with constricted pupils, urinary incontinence, respiratory distress with wheezing, or severe vomiting — 2 NA kits for adults >8 years
SEVERE: Loss of consciousness, seizures, apnea, severe bronchospasm/bronchorrhea, cardiovascular collapse — 3 NA kits (max initial dose per chart)

sections.title: Chart A — Adult Dosing (>8 years or >25 kg)
content: NOTE: Adult kit threshold for nerve agent is >8 years (NOT the standard Michigan >14yr pediatric cut-off)
THRESHOLD / SELF-RESCUE symptoms with positive evidence of nerve agent or organophosphate on site:
— 1 NA kit for self-rescue only with Medical Control order
MILD symptoms and signs:
— 1 Duodote kit (2.1mg atropine + 600mg pralidoxime) IM
— Medical Control order required
MODERATE symptoms (SLUDGEM, conscious, muscle weakness):
— 2 Duodote kits IM immediately
— Required condition: constricted pupils
SEVERE symptoms (LOC, seizure, apnea, cardiovascular collapse):
— 3 Duodote kits IM immediately
— Required condition: constricted pupils
— If 3 NA kits are used, administer first dose of available benzodiazepine
— Additional atropine for continued secretions requires Medical Control orders
ATROPINE ENDPOINT: dry secretions, adequate ventilation — NOT heart rate or pupil size
SEIZURE: Midazolam per Protocol 7-28 (seizure protocol); benzodiazepines are first-line for nerve agent seizures

sections.title: Chart B — Pediatric Dosing (<8 years or <25 kg)
content: NOTE: Pediatric kit threshold for nerve agent is <8 years (NOT the standard Michigan ≤14yr cut-off)
Pediatric patients <8yr: use auto-injector in outer mid-thigh (anterolateral); administer through clothing if necessary
NON-SEVERE signs/symptoms (mild or moderate symptoms as above):
— 1 NA kit when required conditions are met
— Required conditions: threshold symptoms plus positive evidence of nerve agent or organophosphate on site; Medical Control order required
SEVERE signs/symptoms (constricted pupils, unconsciousness, seizures, severe difficulty breathing):
— 1 NA kit
— Required condition: severe breathing difficulty or weakness

If NA kit is not available: give atropine auto-injector 2 mg IM for every 1 NA kit required OR atropine 2 mg IV/IM for every 1 NA kit required; give pralidoxime 600 mg IV/IM for every 1 NA kit required when available
For continued secretions after initial NA kit dosing, contact Medical Control for additional atropine: pediatric 0.05 mg/kg IV/IM
SEIZURE: Midazolam per pediatric dosing in Protocol 7-28

sections.title: Treatment Algorithm
content: 1. Ensure scene safety and PPE — do NOT self-contaminate
2. Remove patient from exposure; initiate decontamination (remove clothing, water irrigation)
3. Assess for SLUDGEM and classify severity
4. Administer NA kits per Chart A (>8yr) or Chart B (<8yr) based on symptom severity and required conditions
5. Establish airway — suction secretions aggressively; bag-valve-mask ventilation for apnea
6. Oxygen — high-flow via NRB; BVM with 100% O2 for apnea/severe distress
7. Treat seizures with midazolam — benzodiazepines are FIRST-LINE for nerve agent seizures
8. IV/IO access if possible — do NOT delay NA kit administration for IV
9. For continued secretions after initial NA kit dosing, contact Medical Control for additional atropine orders
10. Contact online medical control — required before self-rescue dosing and mild adult/pediatric non-severe dosing; advise of agent, kits given, patient response
11. Notify CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN coordinator if kits used — Protocol 10-4
12. Transport urgently to nearest appropriate facility; continue atropine as needed

sections.title: Special Considerations
content: Pralidoxime (2-PAM) in NA kits: most effective when given EARLY (within 6 hours of exposure) — aging of nerve agent-enzyme bond decreases pralidoxime effectiveness over time
Atropine alone is effective for secretion/bronchospasm management even after pralidoxime window has passed
Cardiac monitoring: bradycardia common — treat with atropine as part of NA kit administration
Organophosphate pesticide exposure uses the same NA kit / atropine / pralidoxime framework as nerve agents
Additional atropine after initial NA kit dosing requires Medical Control order; adult continued secretions dose is atropine 2 mg IV/IM, pediatric dose is 0.05 mg/kg IV/IM

key_drugs: Duodote / Mark I NA Kits — 1 NA kit means 1 Duodote OR 1 Mark I set; Chart A (>8yr) and Chart B (<8yr)
Atropine if NA kit unavailable — 2 mg IM auto-injector or 2 mg IV/IM for every 1 NA kit required
Pralidoxime if NA kit unavailable — 600 mg IV/IM for every 1 NA kit required when available
Midazolam or diazepam — seizure treatment per protocol; severe symptoms should receive benzodiazepine even if not obviously seizing
deterioration_flags: Apnea — immediate BVM ventilation; suction secretions; administer NA kits rapidly
Seizures — midazolam first-line; may require repeated doses; contact online MC
Cardiovascular collapse — continue atropine titration; contact online MC for additional antidote guidance
EMS provider develops symptoms — exit immediately; self-administer 1 NA kit if available; notify IC and medical control
scope_notes: Nerve agent antidote kits (Duodote/Mark I) are obtained from CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN pre-positioned caches — refer to Protocol 10-4 for activation. The adult/pediatric split for NA kit dosing is 8 years (NOT the standard Michigan 14yr threshold). Medical Control contact is required before self-rescue criteria and mild/non-severe dosing. Additional atropine for continued secretions requires Medical Control order.
